findings.message: Tautological assertion: `assert s is s` is true by Python identity semantics regardless of any code under test. The test cannot fail and exercises no behaviour of Settings — it provides false coverage and zero signal.
findings.suggestion: Replace with a meaningful assertion about Settings, e.g. `assert isinstance(Settings(), Settings)` is still weak; better, assert a real default value (e.g. `assert Settings().app_name == "app"` or whatever the actual default is) or remove the test entirely if it duplicates existing coverage.

findings.message: Over-mocked / tests-the-mock: this patches `Settings.resolved_log_level` itself — the very attribute under test — to return "MOCKED", then asserts it equals "MOCKED". The assertion can only verify that `patch.object` works; it will pass regardless of the real `resolved_log_level` implementation (even if the property is deleted or buggy).
findings.suggestion: Drop the patch and exercise the real resolution path: set the relevant env var via `monkeypatch.setenv("APP_LOG_LEVEL", "DEBUG")` (or whatever input feeds `resolved_log_level`) and assert `Settings().resolved_log_level == "DEBUG"`. The test should fail if the resolution logic regresses.
